# 'gs cc --fill' sets the shared GS_OPERATION env contract and the
# message-specific GS_MESSAGE_UPDATE for the generator.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-03-30T14:59:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git checkout -b feature
gs branch track --base main

# configure a generator that echoes the env vars as the JSON title
chmod 755 $WORK/gen.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen.sh

# create a commit using --fill
git add foo.txt
gs cc --fill

# verify the env vars were set correctly
git log -1 --format=%s
stdout 'op=commit-create update=false'

-- repo/foo.txt --
Contents of foo.
-- gen.sh --
#!/bin/sh
op="${GS_OPERATION:-}"
update="${GS_MESSAGE_UPDATE:-}"
printf '{"title":"op=%s update=%s"}' "$op" "$update"
